chapter: JEF P101 – A Triumph of Surgery
title: A Triumph of Surgery – Easy VSAQs

questions.question: Who is the author of 'A Triumph of Surgery'?
questions.answer: James Herriot.

questions.question: Who is Tricki?
questions.answer: Tricki is a small pet dog.

questions.question: Who owns Tricki?
questions.answer: Mrs Pumphrey owns Tricki.

questions.question: What problem was Tricki suffering from?
questions.answer: Tricki suffered from illness due to overfeeding.

questions.question: What is the profession of the narrator?
questions.answer: The narrator is a veterinary surgeon.

questions.question: Why was Tricki taken to the surgery?
questions.answer: To control his diet and give him proper exercise.

questions.question: What kind of food was stopped for Tricki?
questions.answer: Rich and fatty food was stopped.

questions.question: Did Tricki undergo surgery?
questions.answer: No, Tricki did not undergo any surgery.

questions.question: How long did Tricki stay at the surgery?
questions.answer: Tricki stayed for about two weeks.

questions.question: What helped Tricki recover?
questions.answer: Proper diet, exercise, and discipline helped Tricki recover.

questions.question: What change was seen in Tricki after treatment?
questions.answer: He became active and energetic.

questions.question: Why is the title ironic?
questions.answer: Because no surgery was actually performed.

questions.question: What quality of the vet cured Tricki?
questions.answer: The vet’s common sense cured Tricki.

questions.question: What did Mrs Pumphrey send to the vet?
questions.answer: She sent eggs, wine, and brandy.

questions.question: What was Mrs Pumphrey’s nature?
questions.answer: She was overindulgent and loving.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: Excessive love without discipline can be harmful.

questions.question: How did Tricki behave at first in the surgery?
questions.answer: He was dull and inactive.

questions.question: Who are the other dogs?
questions.answer: They are dogs at the veterinary surgery.

questions.question: What made Mrs Pumphrey think it was a triumph?
questions.answer: She believed surgery had cured Tricki.

questions.question: What literary device is used in the title?
questions.answer: Irony.

questions.question: What role does humour play in the story?
questions.answer: It makes the message gentle and engaging.

questions.question: Why was exercise important for Tricki?
questions.answer: It helped him lose weight and regain strength.

questions.question: What does Tricki’s recovery symbolise?
questions.answer: The importance of discipline and moderation.

questions.question: How did the vet treat Mrs Pumphrey?
questions.answer: He treated her kindly and patiently.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the prose?
questions.answer: Overindulgence leads to harm.
